column6: Deal Page
column9: Deal Notes

column6: SPAR Group Acquisition by Highwire Capital
column9: Under the agreement approved by SPAR Group’s Board of Directors, stockholders will receive $2.50 per share in cash, reflecting a 72% premium over the last trading day's closing price and a 37.8% premium over the 30-day volume-weighted average share price, leading to SPAR Group becoming a privately held company after stockholder approval and transaction completion, with its stock ceasing to trade on NASDAQ

column6: Surmodics Acquisition by GTCR
column9: GTCR affiliates will acquire all outstanding shares of Surmodics for $43.00 per share, totaling approximately $627 million, which is a 41.1% premium over the 30-day volume-weighted average closing price as of May 28, 2024; the transaction has been unanimously approved by Surmodics’ Board of Directors, who recommend stockholders vote in favor

column6: Adams Resources Acquisition by Tres Energy
column9: Adams Resources ($AE) to be acquired by Tres Energy in an all-cash deal, at $38.00 per share (39% premium to the closing price prior to announcement), with an appx. enterprise value of appx. $139M.

column6: BM Technologies Acquisition by First Carolina Bank
column9: BM Technologies ($BMTX) will be acquired by First Carolina Bank in an all-cash deal valued at approximately $67 million, offering $5.00 per share, which represents a 55.28% premium to the closing price prior to the announcement

column6: Sterling Bancorp Acquisition by EverBank Financial
column9: Sterling Bancor will be acquired by EverBank Financial Corp for $272M in cash, or appx. $5.16 per share. Based on current transaction fees estimates, the payment is expected to be appx. $257M or $4.89 per share.

column6: Heartland Banc Acquisition by German American Bancorp
column9: Under the definitive agreement, Heartland shareholders will receive 3.90 shares of German American common stock for each share of Heartland common stock, with the Heartland retirement plan shares exchanged for cash; German American will issue approximately 7.66 million shares, valuing Heartland shares at $155.37 each based on a $39.84 average price, resulting in an aggregate transaction value of about $330.2 million

column6: Family Dollar Stores (Dollar Tree subsidiary) Acquisition by Dollar Tree
column9: Dollar Tree (NASDAQ: DLTR) has agreed to sell its Family Dollar segment to Brigade Capital Management and Macellum Capital Management for $1.007 billion, subject to customary adjustments. The divestiture follows a strategic review and aims to unlock shareholder value. Family Dollar will remain headquartered in Chesapeake, VA. Dollar Tree originally acquired the brand for $9 billion in 2015. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2025.

column6: Bank of Idaho Acquisition by Glacier Bancorp
column9: Glacier Bancorp (NYSE: GBCI) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Bank of Idaho Holding Co. (OTCQX: BOID) in an all-stock transaction. BOID shareholders will receive 1.100 shares of Glacier stock for each BOID share, with an aggregate consideration of $245.4 million based on Glacier's closing price of $47.70 on January 10, 2025, equating to a value of $52.47 per BOID share. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2025, integrating Bank of Idaho's operations into Glacier's existing divisions.

column6: Liberty TripAdvisor Acquisition by Tripadvisor
column9: Tripadvisor (TRIP) will acquire Liberty TripAdvisor (OTC: LTRPA) in a deal valued at approximately $435 million. Liberty TripAdvisor's Series A and B shares will convert to $0.2567 per share in cash, totaling around $20 million, while its preferred stock will convert to $42.47 million in cash and over 3 million Tripadvisor shares, with the aim to simplify Tripadvisor's capital structure. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2025.

column6: Kellanova Acquisition by Mars
column9: Mars, Incorporated, a family-owned, global leader in pet care, snacking and food, and Kellanova (NYSE: K), a leading company in global snacking, international cereal and noodles, North American plant-based foods and frozen breakfast foods, today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Mars has agreed to acquire Kellanova for $83.50 per share in cash, for a total consideration of $35.9 billion, including assumed net leverage.1 The transaction price represents a premium of approximately 44% to Kellanova’s unaffected 30-trading day volume weighted average price and a premium of approximately 33% to Kellanova’s unaffected 52-week high as of August 2, 2024. The total consideration represents an acquisition multiple of 16.4x LTM adjusted EBITDA as of June 29, 2024.

column6: Spirit AeroSystems Holdings Acquisition by The Boeing Company
column9: Boeing has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Spirit AeroSystems in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $4.7 billion, or $37.25 per share, with a total transaction value of about $8.3 billion including Spirit's net debt; Spirit shareholders will receive between 0.18 and 0.25 Boeing shares per Spirit share based on Boeing's average share price, with a floor of $149.00 and a ceiling of $206.94 per share

column6: Allete Acquisition by CPP Investments (CPPIB), GIP
column9: ALLETE, Inc. (NYSE: ALE) has entered into a definitive agreement with a partnership led by Canada Pension Plan Investment Board and Global Infrastructure Partners, under which the partnership will acquire ALLETE for $67.00 per share in cash, totaling $6.2 billion including the assumption of debt

column6: The AZEK Company Acquisition by James Hardie Industries plc
column9: James Hardie Industries (ASX/NYSE: JHX) will acquire The AZEK Company (NYSE: AZEK) in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at $8.75 billion, including AZEK’s net debt. Under the terms, AZEK shareholders will receive $26.45 in cash and 1.0340 James Hardie shares (NYSE-listed) per AZEK share, implying a total value of $56.88 per share based on James Hardie’s ASX CDI closing price on March 21, 2025. The deal represents a 26% and 21% premium to AZEK’s 30-day and 60-day VWAP, respectively. Post-closing, James Hardie and AZEK shareholders will own 74% and 26% of the combined company. James Hardie’s shares will be listed on both the NYSE and ASX following the transaction, which is expected to close in H2 2025.

column6: BRF Acquisition by Marfrig Global Foods
column9: Marfrig Global Foods S.A. and BRF S.A. have signed a definitive agreement for Marfrig to acquire all BRF shares it does not already own via a share exchange, making BRF a wholly owned subsidiary. BRF shareholders will receive 0.8521 Marfrig shares per BRF share, with fractional shares aggregated and sold on the B3 exchange. BRF shareholders can exercise withdrawal rights at R$9.43 or R$19.89 per share depending on their chosen valuation basis. ADS holders on the NYSE will receive the same exchange ratio. As part of the agreement, BRF shareholders will receive up to R$3.52 billion in dividends, while Marfrig shareholders will receive R$2.5 billion. The merger is subject to approval at both companies’ June 18, 2025 EGMs, regulatory clearance, and no material adverse events. Post-merger, Marfrig intends to rename itself MBRF Global Foods Company S.A.

column6: Emeren Group Acquisition by Shurya Vitra
column9: Emeren has entered into a definitive Merger Agreement to be acquired by Shurya Vitra Ltd. via Merger Sub, with the deal valuing Emeren at $0.20 per ordinary share or $2.00 per ADS (each ADS representing 10 ordinary shares). The transaction will result in Emeren becoming a privately held company, with its shares and ADSs delisted from the NYSE. The acquisition is expected to close in Q3 2025, pending shareholder approval and other customary conditions. Himanshu H. Shah has provided an equity commitment to fund the transaction. The deal was approved by Emeren’s Board of Directors based on a unanimous recommendation from an independent Special Committee.

column6: Heliogen Acquisition by Zeo Energy
column9: Zeo Energy has entered into a definitive all-stock merger agreement to acquire Heliogen in a transaction valuing Heliogen’s equity at approximately $10M, based on Zeo’s Class A common stock price of $1.5859 per share. The final consideration is subject to adjustments based on Heliogen’s net cash at closing. The deal has been unanimously approved by both boards and is expected to close in Q3 2025, pending customary closing conditions and Heliogen shareholder approval. Zeo stockholder approval is not required under Nasdaq rules. Heliogen shareholders representing ~23% of outstanding shares have entered into voting agreements in support of the merger.

column6: LENSAR Acquisition by Alcon
column9: Alcon (NYSE: ALC) announced it will acquire LENSAR (NASDAQ: LNSR) for $14.00 per share in cash, with a contingent value right (CVR) of up to $2.75 per share if 614,000 cumulative procedures are performed using LENSAR’s products between Jan 1, 2026, and Dec 31, 2027. The deal implies a total potential transaction value of approximately $430 million and represents a 24% premium to LENSAR’s 30-day VWAP and 47% to its 90-day VWAP. Lazard advised Alcon; Wells Fargo advised LENSAR. The transaction is expected to close in mid-to-late 2025.

column6: Bridge Investment Group Acquisition by Apollo
column9: Apollo (NYSE: APO) has agreed to acquire Bridge Investment Group Holdings (NYSE: BRDG) in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $1.5 billion. Under the deal, Bridge shareholders will receive 0.07081 shares of Apollo stock per share (valued at $11.50 each), with the transaction expected to close in Q3 2025.

column6: Interpublic Group Acquisition by Omnicom
column9: Announcement (12/9/24): Interpublic Group ($IPG) to be acquired by Omnicom ($OMC) in an all-stock deal, with IPG holders to receive 0.344 OMC shares per IPG share (21.59% premium to the closing price prior to announcement), implying an equity consideration of appx. $13.25 billion at the time of the announcement. IPG and OMC holders will own 39.4% and 60.6% of the combined company, respectively. The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2025 with the combined company retaining the Omnicom name and continuing to trade under the OMC ticker. 12/8/24: Omnicom (OMC), the world's third largest advertising firm, is in advanced talks to acquire Interpublic Group (IPG), the fourth largest, in an all-stock deal that may be valued between $13 billion and $14 billion. The merger, which could be announced as soon as this week, would create the largest advertising company globally with a combined net revenue exceeding $20 billion.

column6: Cross Country Healthcare Acquisition by Aya Healthcare
column9: Cross Country Healthcare ($CCRN) to be acquired by Aya Healthcare in an all-cash deal, at $18.61 per share (67% premium to the closing price prior to announcement), in a transaction valued at appx. $615M. Closing is expected in H1 2025.

column6: scPharmaceuticals Acquisition by MannKind
column9: MannKind Corporation (NASDAQ: MNKD) and scPharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ: SCPH) signed a definitive merger agreement under which MannKind will acquire scPharmaceuticals via a tender offer at $5.35 per share in cash plus a non-tradable contingent value right (CVR) of up to $1.00 per share, tied to regulatory and net sales milestones. The deal values scPharmaceuticals at $303 million upfront and up to $360 million including CVRs, with the upfront price representing a 36% premium to scPharmaceuticals’ 90-day VWAP and total potential consideration reflecting a 31% premium to its August 22, 2025 close. The transaction, expected to close in Q4 2025 pending regulatory approvals and other conditions, requires MannKind to repay scPharmaceuticals’ outstanding debt and buy out Perceptive’s revenue participation rights, totaling about $81 million if closed on September 30, 2025.

column6: Vasta Platform Acquisition by Cogna Educação
column9: COGNA EDUCAÇÃO S.A. (B3: COGN3) launched a tender offer to acquire up to all 15,970,992 outstanding Class A common shares of its controlled subsidiary Vasta Platform Limited (NASDAQ: VSTA) at $5.00 per share, valuing the offer at up to $79.9M. Cogna already holds 64,436,093 Class B shares, representing 97.6% of Vasta’s capital. The offer, approved by Cogna’s board and filed with the SEC, is set to expire October 15, 2025, unless extended. If completed, Vasta will delist from NASDAQ and deregister with the SEC.

column6: ZimVie Acquisition by ARCHIMED
column9: ZimVie Inc. (Nasdaq: ZIMV) has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by healthcare-focused investment firm ARCHIMED for $19.00 per share in cash, representing a 99% premium to ZimVie’s 90-day VWAP and valuing the company at approximately $730 million enterprise value. The transaction, unanimously approved by ZimVie's board, is expected to close by year-end 2025, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals. A 40-day go-shop period runs through August 29, 2025, during which ZimVie may solicit alternative proposals. Upon closing, ZimVie will become a privately held company and delist from NASDAQ. Centerview Partners and UBS are acting as financial advisors to ZimVie and ARCHIMED, respectively.

column6: Tourmaline Bio Acquisition by Novartis
column9: Novartis (NYSE: NVS) has agreed to acquire Tourmaline Bio (NASDAQ: TRML) in a $1.4 billion all-cash deal, paying $48.00 per share, a 59% premium to Tourmaline’s September 8, 2025 close and 127% above its 60-day VWAP. The acquisition centers on pacibekitug, Tourmaline’s late-stage IL-6 inhibitor targeting residual inflammation in cardiovascular disease, where no widely adopted therapies currently exist. Tourmaline’s board, alongside Novartis’, unanimously approved the transaction, which will be executed via a tender offer requiring majority shareholder participation and regulatory approvals. The deal is expected to close in Q4 2025, after which Tourmaline will operate as a Novartis subsidiary until integration. Novartis Chief Medical Officer Shreeram Aradhye emphasized the asset’s potential to transform cardiovascular care, while Tourmaline CEO Sandeep Kulkarni highlighted the compelling value delivered to shareholders and the strategic fit with Novartis’ cardiovascular, renal, and metabolic portfolio.

column6: 89bio Acquisition by Roche
column9: 89bio, Inc. (NASDAQ: ETNB) agreed to be acquired by Roche in a tender offer valuing the company at up to $3.5 billion, with shareholders to receive $14.50 per share in cash at closing—representing a 79% premium to the September 17, 2025 close—plus a non-tradeable CVR of up to $6.00 per share tied to regulatory and commercial milestones for pegozafermin, including $2.00 upon first commercial sale in F4 MASH cirrhosis (by March 31, 2030), $1.50 if annual sales reach $3.0 billion by 2033, and $2.50 if sales reach $4.0 billion by 2035; the transaction, totaling $2.4 billion upfront equity value and up to $3.5 billion on a fully diluted basis, is expected to close in Q4 2025 pending a majority tender, regulatory review, and customary conditions, after which Roche will acquire untendered shares via a second-step merger on identical terms.

column6: HarborOne Bancorp Acquisition by Eastern Bankshares
column9: Eastern Bankshares will acquire HarborOne Bancorp in a stock-and-cash transaction valued at approximately $490 million, based on Eastern’s April 23, 2025, closing price. HarborOne shareholders can elect to receive either 0.765 shares of Eastern common stock or $12.00 in cash per share, subject to proration ensuring 75%-85% stock consideration. The merger, structured to qualify as a tax-free reorganization, is expected to result in the issuance of around 25.2 million Eastern shares and $99 million in cash, enhancing Eastern’s regional banking presence.

column6: Avidity Biosciences Acquisition by Novartis
column9: Avidity Biosciences Inc. (NASDAQ: RNA) entered into a definitive merger agreement to be acquired by Novartis AG for $72.00 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately $12 billion on a fully diluted basis. The offer represents a 46% premium to Avidity’s October 24, 2025 closing price of $49.15 and a 62% premium to its 30-day VWAP. The transaction, unanimously approved by both boards, will occur following the separation of Avidity’s early-stage precision cardiology programs into a new publicly traded company, SpinCo. SpinCo will be led by current Avidity Chief Program Officer Kathleen Gallagher as CEO, with Avidity CEO Sarah Boyce serving as chair of its board. Avidity shareholders will receive additional consideration tied to the SpinCo separation. The acquisition expands Novartis’s RNA therapeutics pipeline, leveraging Avidity’s Antibody Oligonucleotide Conjugate (AOC™) platform to advance treatments for rare and genetic diseases.

column6: Adverum Biotechnologies Acquisition by Eli Lilly
column9: Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Adverum Biotechnologies Inc. (NASDAQ: ADVM) for up to $12.47 per share, comprising $3.56 in cash at closing and a non-transferable contingent value right (CVR) worth up to an additional $8.91 per share tied to future milestones. The CVR will pay up to $1.78 upon U.S. approval of Ixo-vec within seven years of closing and up to $7.13 upon achieving annual worldwide net sales of Ixo-vec exceeding $1 billion within ten years. The acquisition includes Adverum’s lead gene therapy candidate, Ixo-vec, for treating ocular diseases. Lilly will commence a tender offer for all outstanding Adverum shares, with the transaction expected to close in Q4 2025.

column6: Akero Therapeutics Acquisition by Novo Nordisk
column9: Novo Nordisk agreed to acquire Akero Therapeutics (NASDAQ: AKRO) for $54 per share in cash, valuing the deal at $4.7 billion, with an additional non-transferable contingent value right (CVR) of $6 per share (worth up to $0.5 billion) payable upon U.S. regulatory approval of Akero’s lead drug, efruxifermin (EFX), for compensated cirrhosis due to metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH). Akero, a clinical-stage biotech, is advancing EFX through phase 3 trials for patients with moderate to advanced liver fibrosis (F2–F3) and cirrhosis (F4). The acquisition, aimed at strengthening Novo Nordisk’s pipeline in serious metabolic diseases, is expected to close around year-end.

column6: Integral Ad Science Acquisition by Novacap
column9: Integral Ad Science (NASDAQ: IAS) agreed to be acquired by Novacap in an all-cash deal valued at $1.9 billion, with shareholders receiving $10.30 per share, a 22% premium to IAS’s September 23, 2025 close. Current shareholder Vista will exit its stake upon closing. The transaction, unanimously approved by IAS’s board, is expected to close before year-end 2025, after which IAS will continue operating under its existing name and brand while accelerating growth in its AI-first media measurement and optimization platform.

column6: Metsera Acquisition by Pfizer
column9: Pfizer (NYSE: PFE) agreed to acquire Metsera (NASDAQ: MTSR) for $47.50 per share in cash, valuing the clinical-stage obesity and cardiometabolic biotech at $4.9 billion enterprise value, with an additional CVR of up to $22.50 per share tied to three milestones: $5 upon initiation of a Phase 3 trial for the MET-097i+MET-233i combo, $7 upon FDA approval of MET-097i monotherapy, and $10.50 upon FDA approval of the combo therapy. The deal, expected to close in Q4 2025, could deliver up to $70 per share in total consideration if all milestones are achieved.

column6: The ODP Acquisition by Atlas Holdings
column9: The ODP Corporation (NASDAQ: ODP) agreed to be acquired by Atlas Holdings for $28 per share in cash, valuing the deal at about $1 billion and representing a 34% premium to ODP’s September 19, 2025, closing price. The transaction, unanimously approved by ODP’s board, is expected to close by year-end 2025, after which ODP will become a private company and delist from Nasdaq.

column6: PROS Holdings Acquisition by Thoma Bravo
column9: PROS Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: PRO) agreed to be acquired by Thoma Bravo in an all-cash deal valued at $1.4 billion, with shareholders receiving $23.25 per share, a 41.7% premium to PROS’ September 19, 2025 close and a 53.2% premium to its 30-day VWAP; the transaction, unanimously approved by PROS’ board, is expected to close in Q4 2025, after which PROS will become private under Thoma Bravo’s ownership.

column6: Paramount Group Acquisition by Rithm Capital Corp
column9: Rithm Capital Corp. entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Paramount Group, Inc. (NYSE: PGRE) for approximately $1.6B in cash, paying $6.60 per share; Paramount’s portfolio comprises 13 owned and 4 managed Class A office assets totaling over 13.1M square feet in New York City and San Francisco, 85.4% leased as of June 30, 2025, and the transaction, approved by both boards and expected to close in late Q4 2025, will be funded through Rithm’s balance sheet liquidity and potential co-investors.

column6: Vimeo Acquisition by Bending Spoons
column9: Bending Spoons agreed to acquire Vimeo, Inc. (NASDAQ: VMEO) in an all-cash deal valued at approximately $1.38B, with Vimeo shareholders to receive $7.85 per share, representing a 91% premium to the 60-day VWAP as of September 9, 2025; the transaction was unanimously approved by Vimeo’s board and is expected to close in Q4 2025.

column6: Vital Energy Acquisition by Crescent Energy
column9: Crescent Energy (NYSE: CRGY) and Vital Energy (NYSE: VTLE) announced a definitive all-stock merger agreement valued at about $3.1 billion, including debt, creating a top-10 independent E&P with scaled positions and a free cash flow–focused strategy. Under the deal, Vital shareholders will receive 1.9062 Crescent Class A shares for each Vital share, representing a 5% premium to the exchange ratio and a 15% premium to Vital’s 30-day VWAP as of August 22, 2025. Following closing, Crescent shareholders will own ~77% and Vital shareholders ~23% of the combined company. The boards of both companies and a special committee of independent Crescent directors have unanimously approved the transaction, with major shareholders representing ~29% of Crescent and ~20% of Vital agreeing to support it. The merger, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, is expected to close by year-end 2025.

column6: Mural Oncology Acquisition by XOMA Royalty
column9: XOMA Royalty Corporation (Nasdaq: XOMA) has agreed to acquire Mural Oncology plc (Nasdaq: MURA) in an all-cash deal via a High Court-sanctioned Irish scheme of arrangement, with a base price of $2.035 per share, plus up to $0.205 per share in contingent value tied to Mural’s closing net cash. The acquisition implies a base equity value of approximately $36.2 million, with the additional consideration payable only if Mural’s closing cash exceeds the estimated $36.2 million. The offer represents a 13.1% premium to Mural’s August 19, 2025 closing price and a 97.6% premium to its April 14 undisturbed price. The transaction, unanimously approved by both boards, is subject to shareholder and Irish High Court approval and is expected to close by year-end 2025. XOMA will effect the purchase through a wholly owned subsidiary, XRA 5 Corp..

column6: MeridianLink Acquisition by Centerbridge Partners
column9: MeridianLink (NYSE: MLNK) entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by affiliates of Centerbridge Partners in an all-cash transaction valuing the company at an enterprise value of approximately $2.0 billion. Shareholders will receive $20.00 per share in cash, representing a 26% premium to the August 8, 2025, closing price. The transaction, unanimously approved by MeridianLink’s board, is expected to close in the second half of 2025, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, after which MeridianLink will become a private company.

column6: BankFinancial Acquisition by First Financial Bancorp
column9: First Financial (NASDAQ: FFBC) agreed to acquire BankFinancial (NASDAQ: BFIN) in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $142 million, based on First Financial’s August 8, 2025, closing price. BankFinancial shareholders will receive 0.48 First Financial shares for each BankFinancial share, expanding First Financial’s footprint in the Chicagoland market with 18 financial centers and enhancing its commercial banking presence. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to be accretive to EPS with minimal impact on tangible book value per share. Closing is anticipated in Q4 2025, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: Aris Water Solutions Acquisition by Western Midstream Partners
column9: Western Midstream Partners (NYSE: WES) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Aris Water Solutions (NYSE: ARIS) in a cash-and-equity transaction valued at approximately $1.5 billion, with a total enterprise value of $2.0 billion including debt. Aris shareholders will receive 0.625 WES common units per share or may elect $25.00 in cash per share, subject to proration, with total cash consideration capped at $415 million. The deal represents a 10% premium to Aris’s 30-day VWAP and 23% to its August 5, 2025, close. Assuming maximum cash consideration, WES will issue ~26.6 million common units, resulting in a 72% equity / 28% cash mix. Aris shareholders are expected to own ~7% of WES post-close. WES will assume $500 million of Aris’s outstanding senior notes. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards and backed by 42% of Aris shareholders via support agreements, is expected to close in Q4 2025, pending customary approvals.

column6: Steelcase Acquisition by HNI Corp
column9: HNI Corporation (NYSE: HNI) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Steelcase Inc. (NYSE: SCS) in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at approximately \$2.2 billion. Under the terms, Steelcase shareholders will receive \$7.20 in cash and 0.2192 shares of HNI common stock for each Steelcase share, implying a total consideration of \$18.30 per share based on HNI’s closing price of \$50.62 as of August 1, 2025. This reflects a valuation of approximately 5.8x Steelcase’s trailing twelve-month adjusted EBITDA, inclusive of \$120 million in anticipated run-rate cost synergies. Following the closing, HNI shareholders will own 64% and Steelcase shareholders 36% of the combined entity. The deal is expected to close by the end of calendar year 2025, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.

column6: LAVA Therapeutics Acquisition by XOMA Royalty
column9: 10/17/25: Cash portion updated to $1.04; At announcement: XOMA Royalty (NASDAQ: XOMA) and LAVA Therapeutics N.V. (NASDAQ: LVTX) have entered into a definitive agreement under which XOMA Royalty will acquire LAVA through a tender offer expected to commence by August 15, 2025. LAVA shareholders will receive between $1.16 and $1.24 in cash per share, comprising a base price of $1.16 and up to $0.08 in additional consideration, plus a non-transferable contingent value right (CVR). The CVR entitles holders to 75% of the net proceeds from LAVA’s two partnered assets and 75% of any proceeds from the sale or out-licensing of unpartnered programs. The offer is subject to minimum tender conditions (generally 80% of shares), the adoption of shareholder resolutions, minimum cash at closing, and other customary conditions. Upon completion of a subsequent offering period, The transaction is expected to close in Q4 2025.

column6: Smart Share Global Acquisition by Trustar Capital, Smart Share Global Management
column9: Smart Share Global (Nasdaq: EM) has entered into a definitive merger agreement with a consortium of investors, including company executives, to be acquired in a go-private transaction implying a total equity value of approximately \$327 million. Under the agreement, shareholders will receive \$0.625 per ordinary share or \$1.25 per American Depository Share (ADS) in cash, reflecting premiums of 74.8% to the January 3, 2025 closing price and 8.7% to the July 31, 2025 closing price. The transaction will be financed through equity contributions from consortium members, a committed term loan from Bank of China, and rollover equity, and is expected to close in Q4 2025 pending shareholder approval, regulatory clearances, and other conditions. Upon completion, Smart Share Global will become a privately held company and delist from Nasdaq.

column6: City Office REIT Acquisition by Elliott Investment Management
column9: City Office REIT (NYSE: CIO) has agreed to be acquired by MCME Carell Holdings for $7.00 per share in cash, in a transaction valued at approximately $1.1 billion, including debt and preferred equity. The deal represents a 26% premium to the prior closing price and a 39% premium to the 90-day VWAP. The transaction, unanimously approved by City Office’s board, is contingent upon shareholder approval and the sale of its Phoenix portfolio, and is expected to close in Q4 2025. Upon completion, City Office will go private, and its common and preferred shares will be delisted from the NYSE. Ask ChatGPT

column6: TC Bancshares Acquisition by Colony Bankcorp
column9: Colony Bankcorp, Inc. (NYSE: CBAN) will acquire 100% of TC Bancshares, Inc. (OTCQX: TCBC) in a stock-and-cash transaction valued at ~$86.1 million. TCBC shareholders can elect to receive either $21.25 in cash or 1.25 CBAN shares per TCBC share, subject to proration so that 20% of shares receive cash and 80% receive stock. The deal is expected to close in Q4 2025. The combined bank will have ~$3.8B in assets.

column6: Sonim Technologies Acquisition by Social Mobile
column9: Sonim Technologies (NASDAQ: SONM) has entered into a definitive agreement to sell its assets and substantially all current liabilities to Social Mobile for $20 million in an all-cash transaction, which includes a potential $5 million earn-out. The deal has been approved by Sonim’s Board of Directors and is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2025, pending customary shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: Zeekr Acquisition by Geely Automobile Holdings
column9: Zeekr Intelligent Technology (NYSE: ZK) entered into a definitive merger agreement with Geely, under which Geely will acquire all outstanding Zeekr shares and ADSs. Holders will have the option to receive either $2.687 in cash per share ($26.87 per ADS) or 1.23 newly issued Geely shares per share (12.3 Geely shares per ADS). Hong Kong non-professional investors will receive only cash. The cash offer represents an 18.9% premium to Zeekr’s May 6, 2025 close. The transaction will be funded by Geely’s internal resources or debt and is expected to close in Q4 2025, following approval by shareholders and customary conditions.

column6: Core Scientific Acquisition by CoreWeave
column9: CoreWeave (NASDAQ: CRWV) to acquire Core Scientific (NASDAQ: CORZ) in an all-stock merger—Core Scientific shareholders will receive 0.1235 CoreWeave Class A shares per Core Scientific share—expected to close in Q4 2025 pending regulatory and shareholder approvals; implies a ~$9.0 billion equity value based on CoreWeave’s 5-day VWAP, a $20.40 per share value and a 66% premium to Core Scientific’s $12.30 closing price on June 25, 2025; pro forma combined company will have ~1.3 GW gross power capacity with over 1 GW expansion potential, and Core Scientific shareholders will own less than 10% post-close.

column6: Sandstorm Gold Acquisition by Royal Gold
column9: Royal Gold (NASDAQ: RGLD) announced definitive agreements to acquire 100% of both Sandstorm Gold (TSX: SSL, NYSE: SAND) and Horizon Copper (TSXV: HCU) in two separate transactions valued at approximately $3.5B and $196M, respectively. Under the Sandstorm deal, structured as a court-approved plan of arrangement under British Columbia law, Sandstorm shareholders will receive 0.0625 Royal Gold shares per Sandstorm share, implying a 21% premium to the 20-day VWAP and 17% to the July 3 closing price. Royal Gold expects to issue roughly 19M shares to Sandstorm holders, who will own approximately 23% of the combined company on a fully diluted basis. Concurrently, Royal Gold will acquire Horizon Copper for C$2.00/share in cash, representing an 85% premium to its 20-day VWAP. Both transactions are expected to close in Q4 2025.

column6: PB Bankshares Acquisition by Norwood Financial
column9: Norwood Financial (NASDAQ: NWFL) and PB Bankshares (NASDAQ: PBBK) announced a merger under which Presence will merge into Norwood in a cash and stock deal valued at approximately $54.9M. Presence shareholders will receive 80% of consideration in Norwood stock (0.7850 shares per Presence share) and 20% in cash ($19.75 per share), subject to proration. The transaction reflects 106.6% of tangible book value and a 2.3% core deposit premium. The combined bank will have ~$3B in assets across Pennsylvania. The deal is expected to close in late Q4 2025 or early Q1 2026.

column6: MRC Global Acquisition by DNOW
column9: DNOW Inc. (NYSE: DNOW) and MRC Global Inc. (NYSE: MRC) have announced an all-stock merger valued at approximately $1.5 billion, combining their complementary energy and industrial supply businesses to form a premier global solutions provider with a $3.0 billion enterprise value. The combined company, to be led by DNOW’s executive team and headquartered in Houston, expects $70 million in annual cost synergies, strong cash flow, and double-digit EPS accretion in year one, with closing anticipated in Q4 2025 pending shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: Guild Acquisition by Bayview Asset Management
column9: Mortgage lender Guild agreed to be taken private by a Bayview Asset Management fund for $20.00 cash per share (~$1.3B equity value), a 56% premium to the 23-May close and 27% above tangible book; a special dividend of up to $0.25/share may be paid before close. Guild will operate alongside Bayview affiliate Lakeview Loan Servicing, keep its management team and strategy, and expects the all-cash deal (no financing condition) to close in Q4 2025, pending customary approvals.

column6: Cantaloupe Acquisition by 365 Retail Markets
column9: Cantaloupe, Inc. (NASDAQ: CTLP) has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by 365 Retail Markets, a leader in unattended retail technology and a portfolio company of Providence Equity Partners. The all-cash deal values Cantaloupe at approximately $848 million, with shareholders receiving $11.20 per share—a 34% premium to its unaffected price. The transaction aims to integrate Cantaloupe’s payments and telemetry software with 365’s self-checkout innovations to serve foodservice and non-foodservice operators globally. Upon closing in the second half of 2025, Cantaloupe will become a private company and its shares will be delisted. The deal is unanimously approved by Cantaloupe's board and backed by committed financing, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: CureVac Acquisition by BioNTech
column9: BioNTech SE (Nasdaq: BNTX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire all outstanding shares of CureVac N.V. (Nasdaq: CVAC) in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $1.25 billion.Each CureVac share will convert into $5.46 of BioNTech ADSs—a 55 percent premium to CureVac’s three-month VWAP—with the final ratio adjusted via a collar tied to BioNTech’s 10-day VWAP before closing. Approved unanimously by both boards, the deal aims to bolster BioNTech’s oncology pipeline by integrating CureVac’s mRNA platform, Tübingen manufacturing site, and research assets. Supported by shareholders holding over 50 percent of CureVac’s stock—led by dievini Hopp BioTech and KfW—the merger, subject to regulatory approval and at least 80 percent acceptance (possibly 75 percent), is slated to close in 2025.

column6: Provident Bancorp Acquisition by NB Bancorp
column9: NB Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq: NBBK), holding company for Needham Bank, has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Provident Bancorp, Inc. (Nasdaq: PVBC), parent of BankProv, in a stock and cash transaction valued at approximately $211.8 million based on Needham’s closing share price of $16.62 on June 4, 2025. Provident shareholders may elect to receive either 0.691 shares of Needham stock or $13.00 in cash per share, subject to allocation procedures ensuring 50% stock consideration. The deal, approved unanimously by both boards, is intended to qualify as a tax-free reorganization and is expected to close in Q4 2025, pending regulatory and shareholder approvals. Following the transaction, BankProv will merge into Needham Bank, and Provident CEO Joseph B. Reilly will join the board. The combined institution will hold $7.1 billion in assets and operate 18 branches across Massachusetts and New Hampshire.

column6: Signing Day Sports Reverse Merger with BlockchAIn Digital Infrastructure
column9: Signing Day Sports has signed a definitive agreement for a reverse merger with One Blockchain, the operating affiliate of blockchAIn Digital Infrastructure, in a reverse merger valued at approximately $215M in equity. The transaction will create a new publicly traded holding company, BlockchAIn Digital Infrastructure (PubCo), listed on NYSE American, with One Blockchain shareholders receiving ~91.5% of the combined entity and Signing Day Sports shareholders ~8.5%, pre-fees. One Blockchain reported $26.8M in revenue and $5.7M in net income for 2024. The merger consideration includes an earnout of 11.628% in additional PubCo shares if 2026 EBITDA meets or exceeds $25M. The deal is structured with no cash consideration and will be led by One Blockchain’s CEO Jerry Tang. Closing is expected in H2 2025, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: CFSB Bancorp Acquisition by Hometown Financial Group
column9: Hometown Financial Group, parent of bankESB and other regional banks, will acquire CFSB Bancorp (Nasdaq: CFSB) and its subsidiary Colonial Federal Savings Bank in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $44 million. CFSB shareholders will receive $14.25 per share in cash. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to close in Q4 2025, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals.

column6: MAG Silver Corp. Acquisition by Pan American Silver Corp
column9: Pan American Silver, a Toronto- and New York-listed precious-metals producer, will acquire all outstanding shares of MAG Silver in a US$2.1 billion plan of arrangement. MAG holds a 44% interest in the high-grade Juanicipio silver mine in Mexico—operated by Fresnillo plc, which owns the remaining 56%—making it a tier-one primary silver producer. Under the agreement, MAG shareholders will receive US$500 million in cash and 0.755 Pan American shares for each MAG share (subject to proration), valuing MAG at US$20.54 per share—a 21% premium to the May 9 closing price and a 27% premium to its 20-day VWAP. Upon closing, expected in H2 2025, former MAG investors will own approximately 14% of Pan American on a fully diluted basis, joining a larger, diversified silver and gold producer.

column6: TaskUs Acquisition by Blackstone, TaskUs Co-Founders
column9: TaskUs (Nasdaq: TASK), a digital services and customer-experience outsourcing firm, will go private after an affiliate of Blackstone and co-founders/CEOs Bryce Maddock and Jaspar Weir agree to acquire all remaining Class A shares at $16.50 each in cash—a 26 % premium to the 30-day VWAP. The buyout is expected to close in H2 2025.

column6: Mesa Air Group Acquisition by Republic Airways
column9: Republic Airways and Mesa Air Group announced a definitive agreement to merge in an all-stock reverse merger transaction, creating a publicly traded regional airline under the name Republic Airways Holdings Inc. The combined company will remain NASDAQ-listed under the ticker "RJET." Republic shareholders will own 88% of the combined entity, while Mesa shareholders will own between 6% and 12%, contingent on Mesa meeting certain pre-closing conditions. All existing Mesa debt will be extinguished as part of the transaction. The merger has received unanimous board approval from both companies and is expected to close in Q3 or Q4 2025.

column6: Shutterstock Acquisition by Getty Images
column9: Getty Images (NYSE: GETY) and Shutterstock (NYSE: SSTK) have agreed to merge, forming a visual content company with an enterprise value of about $3.7 billion. The combined entity will maintain the Getty Images name and trading symbol (GETY). Shutterstock shareholders can elect to receive one of three options: (a) to receive $28.84870 in cash per share; (b) 13.67237 shares of Getty Images stock per share; or (c) a combination of 9.17 shares of Getty Images and $9.50 in cash per share. The total consideration includes $331 million in cash and 319.4 million shares of Getty Images stock. Upon completion, Getty Images shareholders will hold approximately 54.7% and Shutterstock shareholders will hold about 45.3% of the combined entity.

column6: TELUS International Acquisition by TELUS
column9: TELUS Corporation (TSX: T, NYSE: TU) has agreed to acquire all outstanding shares of TELUS International (TSX: TIXT, NYSE: TIXT) that it does not already own for US$4.50 per share, valuing the deal at US$539 million and the company’s equity at roughly US$1.3 billion, with a total transaction value of US$2.9 billion including voting shares. Shareholders can elect cash, TELUS stock, or a mix, though share consideration will be capped at 25% of the total. The price reflects a 52% premium to TIXT’s unaffected June 11 price, a 62.6% premium to the 30-day VWAP, and a 32% increase from TELUS’s initial $3.40 bid. The plan of arrangement has unanimous approval from TIXT’s board (with conflicted directors abstaining) and a special committee, and it is backed by major shareholder EQT, which controls 37.7% of subordinate voting shares. Conditions include shareholder approval at an October 27, 2025 special meeting, court and regulatory approvals, and standard closing conditions, with an outside date of January 2, 2026.

column6: TrueCar Acquisition by Fair Holdings (Scott Palmer)
column9: TrueCar, Inc. (NASDAQ: TRUE) announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Fair Holdings, Inc., an entity led by TrueCar founder Scott Painter, in an all-cash, go-private transaction valued at $2.55 per share, representing an equity value of approximately $227 million based on current shares outstanding. The deal is expected to close in Q4 2025 or early 2026, subject to customary conditions, including a 30-day go-shop period ending November 13, 2025. Financing for the acquisition includes an equity commitment from an affiliate of a major dealer group and cash from TrueCar’s balance sheet, with plans to raise additional capital from a Syndicate prior to closing. The buyer will owe a reverse termination fee if it fails to complete the transaction, though completion remains contingent on securing an additional $60 million in financing commitments.

column6: Cool Company Acquisition by EPS Ventures
column9: Cool Company Ltd. (CoolCo) announced it has entered into a definitive merger agreement with EPS Ventures Ltd (“EPS”), under which EPS will acquire all outstanding CoolCo shares it does not already own for $9.65 per share in cash, representing a 26% premium to the September 22, 2025 close and a 38% premium to the 90-day VWAP. The deal will be executed through a merger of an EPS subsidiary into CoolCo, leaving the company wholly owned by EPS. An independent Special Committee of CoolCo’s board, advised by its own legal and financial counsel, unanimously determined the terms to be fair and recommended shareholder approval. EPS, which already owns 59.3% of CoolCo, has agreed to vote in favor of the merger. The transaction, pending shareholder and customary approvals, is expected to close in Q4 2025 or Q1 2026.

column6: Verint Systems Acquisition by Thoma Bravo
column9: Verint Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNT), The CX Automation Company, has agreed to be acquired by private equity firm Thoma Bravo in an all-cash transaction valued at $2 billion. Under the deal, Verint shareholders will receive $20.50 per share in cash, representing an 18% premium to the company’s unaffected 10-day volume weighted average share price as of June 25, 2025, the day before media reports of a potential sale. The transaction has been unanimously approved by Verint’s board and is structured as a reverse-triangular merger with a Thoma Bravo-controlled entity as parent. The acquisition, which is not subject to a financing condition, remains contingent on shareholder and regulatory approvals and is expected to close before the end of Verint’s current fiscal year.

column6: Guess? Acquisition by Authentic Brands
column9: Guess? Inc. (NYSE: GES) announced a take-private deal valued at approximately $1.4 billion, led by co-founders Maurice and Paul Marciano, CEO Carlos Alberini, and Authentic Brands Group, under which public shareholders will receive $16.75 per share in cash, representing a 73% premium to the company’s unaffected March 14, 2025 closing price. As part of the transaction, Authentic will acquire 51% of Guess?’s intellectual property, while the Rolling Stockholders will retain 49% of the IP and continue to own 100% of the operating company, with current management remaining in place. The transaction, expected to close in Q4 FY2026, is not subject to a financing condition and will be funded through a combination of rollover equity and cash from Authentic, with Apollo Funds providing a hybrid capital solution.

column6: Informatica Inc. Acquisition by Salesforce
column9: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) will acquire the remaining shares of Informatica (NYSE: INFA) it does not already own in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $8 billion. Informatica stockholders will receive $25 per share. The deal has been approved by both companies’ boards and by shareholders representing roughly 63% of Informatica’s voting power. The acquisition, aimed at strengthening Salesforce’s data infrastructure to support agentic AI initiatives, will be financed with cash and new debt and is expected to close in early fiscal year 2027.

column6: Sapiens Acquisition by Advent International
column9: Sapiens International Corporation (NASDAQ: SPNS; TASE: SPNS) entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Advent in an all-cash transaction valuing the company at approximately $2.5 billion. Shareholders will receive $43.50 per share, a 64% premium to Sapiens’ August 8, 2025, undisturbed closing price of $26.52 and a ~51% premium to its 30- and 60-day VWAPs. Upon closing, expected in Q4 2025 or Q1 2026, Sapiens will become a privately held company and its shares will be delisted. Advent has secured committed debt and equity financing, including a $1.3 billion equity contribution from funds it advises.

column6: Hanesbrands Acquisition by Gildan Activewear
column9: Gildan Activewear (TSX: GIL; NYSE: GIL) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire HanesBrands (NYSE: HBI) in a cash-and-stock transaction valuing HanesBrands at an equity value of approximately $2.2 billion and enterprise value of $4.4 billion. HanesBrands shareholders will receive 0.102 Gildan shares and $0.80 in cash per share, implying a total value of $6.00 per share based on August 11, 2025, closing prices—a 24% premium. Upon closing, expected in late 2025 or early 2026, HanesBrands shareholders will own ~19.9% of Gildan on a non-diluted basis. The consideration mix is approximately 87% stock and 13% cash, with Gildan also planning to refinance ~$2 billion of HanesBrands debt. Gildan has secured $2.3 billion in committed financing, including a $1.2 billion bridge facility and $1.1 billion in term loans.

column6: WideOpenWest Acquisition by DigitalBridge, Crestview Partners
column9: WideOpenWest (NYSE: WOW) entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by affiliated investment funds of DigitalBridge Investments and Crestview Partners, its largest shareholder, in an all-cash transaction valuing the company at an enterprise value of approximately $1.5 billion. Shareholders will receive $5.20 per share, representing a 37.2% premium to the unaffected price prior to the initial $4.80 offer in May 2024 and a 63% premium to the August 8, 2025, close. Crestview will roll over all of its existing shares. The deal is expected to close by late 2025 or early 2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals, after which WOW! will be privately held and delisted.

column6: Frontier Communications Acquisition by Verizon Communications
column9: Verizon Communications Inc. has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Frontier Communications Parent, Inc. for $20 billion in an all-cash transaction, valued at $38.50 per share, representing a 43.7% premium to Frontier’s 90-day volume-weighted average share price; this acquisition will enhance Verizon's fiber footprint, integrating Frontier's 2.2 million fiber subscribers and 7.2 million fiber locations into Verizon's existing 7.4 million Fios connections, while Frontier has invested $4.1 billion in its fiber network over the past four years and plans to build an additional 2.8 million fiber locations by the end of 2026

column6: Plymouth Industrial REIT Acquisition by Makarora Management, Ares Alternative Credit
column9: Plymouth Industrial REIT Inc. (NYSE: PLYM) entered into a definitive merger agreement to be acquired by entities affiliated with Makarora Management LP and Ares Alternative Credit funds in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $2.1 billion, including assumed debt. Under the terms, Plymouth shareholders and limited partners of Plymouth Industrial OP, LP will receive $22.00 per share, representing a 50% premium to Plymouth’s August 18, 2025 unaffected closing price, the day before Sixth Street Partners disclosed a competing proposal. The agreement includes a 30-day “go-shop” period ending November 23, 2025, allowing Plymouth to solicit superior offers. Upon closing, Plymouth will become a private company, delist from the NYSE, and deregister under the Exchange Act. The company will continue to pay its previously declared Q3 2025 dividend and any subsequent distributions necessary to maintain REIT status but will suspend other dividends until the transaction closes.

column6: Middlefield Banc Acquisition by Farmers National Banc
column9: Farmers National Banc Corp. (NASDAQ: FMNB) and Middlefield Banc Corp. (NASDAQ: MBCN) signed a definitive all-stock merger agreement under which Middlefield will merge into Farmers, with each Middlefield share exchanged for 2.6 Farmers shares. Based on Farmers’ October 20, 2025 closing price of $13.91, the deal values Middlefield at approximately $299 million, or $36.17 per share, and is structured as a tax-free reorganization. The combined company will form a leading Midwest community banking franchise with over $7 billion in total assets and 83 branches across Northeast and Central Ohio and Western Pennsylvania. The merger, expected to close by the end of Q1 2026.

column6: Avadel Pharmaceuticals Acquisition by Alkermes
column9: Alkermes plc (NASDAQ: ALKS) agreed to acquire Avadel Pharmaceuticals plc (NASDAQ: AVDL) for up to $20.00 per share in cash, valuing Avadel at approximately $2.1 billion. The consideration includes $18.50 per share payable at closing and a non-transferable contingent value right (CVR) of up to $1.50 per share tied to FDA approval of LUMRYZ™ (sodium oxybate) for idiopathic hypersomnia by the end of 2028. The deal represents a 38% premium to Avadel’s three-month volume-weighted average price and 12% to its October 21, 2025 close. The transaction, approved by both boards and expected to close in Q1 2026, will add LUMRYZ™—approved for narcolepsy-related cataplexy and excessive daytime sleepiness—to Alkermes’ portfolio, accelerating its entry into sleep medicine and complementing its neurological pipeline. Alkermes plans to fund the acquisition with cash and new debt, projecting immediate accretion and enhanced profitability post-close.

column6: Hologic Acquisition by TPG, Blackstone
column9: Hologic Inc. (NASDAQ: HOLX) entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Blackstone and TPG in a transaction valued at up to $18.3 billion, or $79 per share, comprising $76 in cash plus a non-tradable contingent value right (CVR) of up to $3 per share tied to revenue milestones in fiscal 2026 and 2027 for the company’s Breast Health division. The deal represents a 46% premium to Hologic’s May 23, 2025 closing price prior to media reports of a possible sale and includes minority investments from Abu Dhabi Investment Authority and GIC. The transaction, expected to close in the first half of 2026, will take Hologic private and support its growth strategy in women’s health.

column6: Hillenbrand Acquisition by Lone Star Funds
column9: Hillenbrand, Inc. (NYSE: HI) announced a definitive agreement to be acquired by an affiliate of Lone Star Funds in an all-cash transaction valued at $32.00 per share, representing an enterprise value of approximately $3.8 billion. The purchase price reflects a 37% premium to Hillenbrand’s August 12, 2025 closing price and a 53% premium to its 90-day VWAP. Hillenbrand, a global provider of highly engineered processing equipment and solutions through its Advanced Process Solutions and Molding Technology Solutions segments, has undergone significant portfolio transformation over the past three years through targeted acquisitions, divestitures, and expansion into industrial food equipment. The transaction, which remains subject to customary approvals and closing conditions, is expected to close by the end of Q1 2026.

column6: Astria Therapeutics Acquisition by BioCryst Pharmaceuticals
column9: BioCryst Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: BCRX) announced a definitive agreement to acquire Astria Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: ATXS) in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at approximately $700 million, or $13.00 per Astria share, representing a 53% premium to Astria’s October 13, 2025 closing price and a 71% premium to its 20-day VWAP. Under the terms, Astria shareholders will receive $8.55 in cash and 0.59 BioCryst shares per Astria share, based on BioCryst’s 20-day VWAP of $7.54. Following the merger, Astria shareholders will own about 15% of the combined company’s equity. BioCryst, which retired its Pharmakon debt on October 8, 2025, has secured a $550 million strategic financing facility from Blackstone to partially fund the cash consideration, alongside existing cash. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to close in Q1 2026, pending regulatory and shareholder approvals.

column6: Heidrick & Struggles Acquisition by Advent, Corvex
column9: Heidrick & Struggles International (Nasdaq: HSII), a leadership advisory and talent solutions provider, has agreed to be acquired by a consortium led by Advent International and Corvex Private Equity, alongside family offices and significant participation from Heidrick leadership, in an all-cash deal valuing the firm at approximately $1.3 billion. Shareholders will receive $59.00 per share, a 26% premium to the company’s 90-day VWAP. The transaction, unanimously approved by Heidrick’s board, is expected to close by Q1 2026, after which the company will become privately held and delist from Nasdaq. Advent, Corvex, and their partners have secured committed financing from Deutsche Bank, UBS, and Santander, with the move designed to accelerate growth, deepen equity participation among Heidrick leaders, and enhance client impact, while the firm continues to operate under its existing brand from its Chicago headquarters.

column6: Merus Acquisition by Genmab
column9: Genmab A/S (Nasdaq: GMAB) agreed to acquire Merus N.V. (Nasdaq: MRUS) in an all-cash tender offer at $97.00 per share, valuing the clinical-stage biotech at $8.0 billion, a 41% premium to Merus’ prior close, with closing targeted for early Q1 2026; the deal, unanimously approved by both boards, will add Merus’ late-stage oncology asset petosemtamab to Genmab’s pipeline, accelerating its shift to a fully owned model and supporting multiple drug launches by 2027, while remaining Merus shareholders post-offer will receive either equal consideration or a court-determined fair price, subject to Dutch statutory proceedings, and financing will be provided through cash on hand and $5.5 billion in committed debt from Morgan Stanley.

column6: First Savings Financial Group Acquisition by First Merchants Corp
column9: First Merchants Corporation (Nasdaq: FRME) and First Savings Financial Group (Nasdaq: FSFG) signed a definitive all-stock merger agreement valuing First Savings at about $241.3 million. Shareholders of First Savings will receive 0.85 shares of First Merchants common stock for each share of First Savings, implying $33.60 per share based on First Merchants’ September 24, 2025 closing price of $39.53. Following completion, First Savings Bank will merge into First Merchants Bank. The deal is expected to be accretive to EPS by 11% in 2027, with a 3-year tangible book value earnback period, and is slated to close in Q1 2026.

column6: Premier Acquisition by Patient Square Capital
column9: Premier (NASDAQ: PINC) entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Patient Square Capital for $28.25 per share in cash, a 23.8% premium to its 60-day VWAP as of September 5, 2025, valuing the deal at roughly $3.4 billion. The transaction, unanimously approved by Premier’s board, is expected to close by Q1 2026, after which Premier will become a private company and delist from Nasdaq. In connection with the acquisition, Premier declared a $0.21 per share dividend payable September 15, 2025, but will suspend future dividends.

column6: Berry Acquisition by California Resources
column9: California Resources Corporation (NYSE: CRC) and Berry Corporation (NASDAQ: BRY) entered into a definitive all-stock merger agreement valuing Berry at approximately $717M including net debt, with Berry shareholders to receive 0.0718 CRC shares per BRY share, a 15% premium to September 12, 2025 prices; the deal gives CRC shareholders about 94% ownership of the combined company, which will have an implied enterprise value over $6B, with CRC planning to refinance Berry’s debt through cash, credit facilities, and potentially new issuance, and the transaction expected to close in Q1 2026.

column6: Dayforce Take Private by Thoma Bravo
column9: Dayforce, Inc. (NYSE:DAY; TSX:DAY) has entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Thoma Bravo in an all-cash transaction that will take the human capital management (HCM) technology company private at an enterprise value of $12.3 billion. Under the deal, Dayforce shareholders will receive $70.00 per share in cash, representing a 32% premium over its closing share price on August 15, 2025—the day before media speculation about a potential acquisition surfaced. The transaction also includes a significant minority investment from the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA). Approved unanimously by Dayforce’s Board of Directors, the deal is expected to close in early 2026, pending stockholder and regulatory approvals, and is not subject to a financing condition. Following the close, Dayforce will operate under its existing brand, but its common stock will be delisted from public exchanges.

column6: American Woodmark Acquisition by MasterBrand
column9: MasterBrand (NYSE: MBC) and American Woodmark (NASDAQ: AMWD) have entered into a definitive all-stock merger agreement that will combine the two cabinetry companies into a single entity with a pro forma equity value of $2.4 billion and an enterprise value of $3.6 billion, based on share prices as of August 5, 2025. Under the terms, American Woodmark shareholders will receive 5.150 shares of MasterBrand common stock for each share they own, resulting in MasterBrand and American Woodmark shareholders owning approximately 63% and 37% of the combined company, respectively. The merger is projected to deliver a trailing 12-month adjusted EBITDA of $639 million, including approximately $90 million in anticipated run rate cost synergies by the end of year three. The transaction, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to close in early 2026 pending shareholder and regulatory approvals and customary conditions.

column6: Synovus Acquisition by Pinnacle Financial
column9: Pinnacle Financial Partners (NASDAQ: PNFP) and Synovus Financial Corp. (NYSE: SNV) will merge in an $8.6 billion all-stock deal to form the Southeast’s highest-return regional bank. The combined company, retaining the Pinnacle name and brand, will be led by Synovus CEO Kevin Blair as CEO and Pinnacle CEO Terry Turner as Chairman. Shareholders of Pinnacle and Synovus will own approximately 51.5% and 48.5% of the combined company, respectively. The fixed exchange ratio of 0.5237 Synovus shares per Pinnacle share implies a 10% premium to Synovus' unaffected price. The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2026 and be over 20% accretive to Pinnacle’s EPS in 2027 with a 2.6-year tangible book value earnback. The new institution will be headquartered in Atlanta and Nashville, operating with a highly aligned leadership structure and local market focus across a high-growth Southeast footprint.

column6: Potlatchdeltic Acquisition by Rayonier
column9: Rayonier (NYSE: RYN) and PotlatchDeltic (Nasdaq: PCH) announced a definitive all-stock merger of equals that will create one of North America’s largest publicly traded timberland and wood products companies, with a pro forma equity market capitalization of $7.1 billion and an enterprise value of $8.2 billion, including $1.1 billion of net debt. Under the terms of the agreement, PotlatchDeltic shareholders will receive 1.7339 Rayonier shares for each PotlatchDeltic share, implying a $44.11 per-share value and an 8.25% premium to PotlatchDeltic’s October 10, 2025 closing price. Post-merger, Rayonier shareholders will own 54% and PotlatchDeltic shareholders 46% of the combined entity, which will operate under a new name to be announced before closing.The transaction, is expected to close in late Q1 or early Q2 2026.

column6: Air Lease Acquisition by Sumitomo Corporation, SMBC Aviation Capital, Apollo, Brookfield
column9: Sumitomo Corporation, SMBC Aviation Capital, Apollo-managed funds, and Brookfield announced a definitive agreement to acquire Air Lease Corporation (NYSE: AL) for $65.00 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately $7.4 billion in equity, or $28.2 billion including debt. Upon closing, Air Lease will be renamed Sumisho Air Lease, with Apollo and Brookfield providing capital support. The offer represents a 7% premium over Air Lease’s all-time high closing stock price on Aug. 28, 2025, a 14% premium over its 30-day VWAP, and a 31% premium over its 12-month VWAP. The deal includes $12.1 billion in committed financing from SMBC, Citi, and Goldman Sachs Bank USA, and Sumisho Air Lease is expected to secure investment-grade ratings from S&P, Fitch, and Kroll. The Air Lease Board unanimously approved the agreement, and the transaction is slated to close in the first half of 2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals, with directors and executives already committed to voting their shares in favor.

column6: Aspen Insurance Acquisition by Sompo Holdings
column9: Sompo Holdings (TSE: 8630) announced that its subsidiary Sompo International will acquire Aspen Insurance Holdings (NYSE: AHL) in a $3.5 billion all-cash deal, paying $37.50 per share. The price represents a 35.6% premium to Aspen’s unaffected share price of $27.66 on August 19, 2025, and a 24.6% premium to its 30-day VWAP prior to deal speculation. Aspen, which writes over $4.6 billion in annual gross premiums across specialty insurance and reinsurance lines, will continue to operate its preference shares unchanged, though Sompo and Aspen may later consider redemption, repurchase, or delisting. The transaction was unanimously approved by both boards and is expected to close in the first half of 2026, subject to customary conditions and approvals.

column6: International Money Express Acquisition by Western Union
column9: Western Union (NYSE: WU) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire International Money Express (NASDAQ: IMXI) in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $500 million, or $16.00 per share. The price represents a ~50% premium to Intermex’s 90-day VWAP. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to close in mid-2026 pending shareholder and regulatory approvals, including HSR clearance. Western Union projects the transaction will be immediately accretive to adjusted EPS by over $0.10 in the first full year post-close and deliver ~$30 million in annual run-rate cost synergies within 24 months, with additional revenue synergies from integrating Intermex’s network and capabilities.

column6: STAAR Surgical Acquisition by Alcon
column9: Alcon (NYSE: ALC) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire STAAR Surgical (NASDAQ: STAA) for $28 per share in cash, valuing the transaction at approximately $1.5 billion. The deal, unanimously approved by both companies’ boards, represents a 59% premium to STAAR’s 90-day VWAP and a 51% premium to its August 4, 2025 closing price. Alcon will acquire STAAR’s EVO Implantable Collamer® Lens product line for correcting moderate to high myopia, with or without astigmatism. The acquisition will be financed through short- and long-term credit facilities and is expected to close within 6–12 months, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. The transaction is projected to be accretive to Alcon’s earnings in year two.

column6: Chart Industries Acquisition by Baker Hughes
column9: Baker Hughes (NASDAQ: BKR) will acquire Chart Industries (NYSE: GTLS) in an all-cash transaction valued at $13.6 billion, paying $210 per share, with the deal expected to close by mid-2026. The acquisition enhances Baker Hughes’ Industrial & Energy Technology segment with Chart’s gas and liquid handling technologies, expanding offerings in LNG, data centers, and decarbonization. The deal is expected to generate $325 million in annual synergies within three years and deliver immediate EPS, margin, and cash flow accretion. Both Boards have unanimously approved the transaction.

column6: TXNM Energy Acquisition by Blackstone Infrastructure Partners
column9: TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) announced it will be acquired by Blackstone Infrastructure for $61.25 per share in cash, representing a total enterprise value of $11.5 billion including net debt and preferred stock. The price reflects a 23% premium to TXNM’s 30-day VWAP as of March 5, 2025, the day before media reports of a potential acquisition. As part of the deal, Blackstone will also invest $400 million through a private placement of 8 million newly issued TXNM shares at $50 per share, expected to close in June 2025. TXNM also plans to raise an additional $400 million in equity prior to deal completion. The acquisition will be funded through equity and assumption of existing debt, with no new debt issuance. The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026.

column6: ProAssurance Acquisition by The Doctors Company
column9: The Doctors Company, the largest physician-owned medical malpractice insurer, has agreed to acquire ProAssurance Corporation (NYSE: PRA) for $25.00 per share in cash, valuing the company at $1.3 billion. The price represents a 60% premium to ProAssurance’s closing share price on March 18, 2025. ProAssurance specializes in medical liability, products liability for medical technology and life sciences, and workers’ compensation insurance. The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

column6: fuboTV Acquisition by Hulu (Disney subsidiary)
column9: FuboTV Inc. (NYSE: FUBO) and The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) have agreed to merge Disney's Hulu + Live TV with Fubo, creating a combined virtual MVPD company. The combined company will retain the Fubo name and FUBO symbol. Disney will own 70% of the new entity, with Fubo holders owning the remaining 30%. Fubo will receive a $220 million cash payment from Disney, FOX, and Warner Bros. Discovery, along with a $145 million term loan commitment from Disney in 2026. A $130 million termination fee will be payable to Fubo under certain circumstances, including failure to receive regulatory approval. Anticipated closing in 12-18 months.

column6: Veeco Instruments Acquisition by Axcelis Technologies
column9: Axcelis Technologies (Nasdaq: ACLS) and Veeco Instruments (Nasdaq: VECO) announced a definitive all-stock merger valued at approximately $4.4 billion, creating a leading semiconductor equipment company with complementary market exposure and expanded scale. Veeco shareholders will receive 0.3575 Axcelis shares for each Veeco share, resulting in pro forma ownership of roughly 58% Axcelis shareholders and 42% Veeco shareholders. On a 2024 pro forma basis, the combined company generated $1.7 billion in revenue, 44% non-GAAP gross margin, and $387 million in adjusted EBITDA, excluding anticipated synergies. The merger, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to enhance R&D capabilities, broaden the product portfolio, and deliver cost and revenue synergies. The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026.

column6: NorthWestern Energy Group Acquisition by Black Hills
column9: Nexstar Media Group (NASDAQ: NXST) is in advanced talks to acquire Tegna (NYSE: TGNA), according to a source familiar with the matter. Tegna shares surged 30% in after-hours trading following the report, while Nexstar’s shares were largely unchanged. A deal could be finalized soon, pending resolution of any last-minute issues, though terms have not yet been disclosed.

column6: MidWestOne Financial Acquisition by Nicolet Bankshares
column9: Nicolet Bankshares Inc. (NYSE: NIC) agreed to acquire MidWestOne Financial Group Inc. (NASDAQ: MOFG) and its wholly owned subsidiary MidWestOne Bank in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $864 million, or $41.37 per share, based on Nicolet’s October 22, 2025 closing price of $130.31. Under the terms, MidWestOne shareholders will receive 0.3175 Nicolet shares for each MidWestOne share owned. The deal values MidWestOne at 166% of tangible book value and 11.5× estimated 2026 earnings. Upon closing, MidWestOne shareholders will own roughly 30% of the combined company, which will have pro forma assets of $15.3 billion, deposits of $13.1 billion, and loans of $11.3 billion, with more than 110 branches and loan offices across the Upper Midwest, Colorado, and Florida. The transaction, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to strengthen Nicolet’s regional presence and scale in key growth markets.

column6: Anywhere Real Estate Acquisition by Compass
column9: Compass (NYSE: COMP) and Anywhere Real Estate (NYSE: HOUS) signed a definitive all-stock merger agreement valuing the combined company at approximately $10 billion enterprise value, including debt assumption. Under the terms, Anywhere shareholders will receive 1.436 Compass Class A shares per HOUS share, equating to $13.01 per share based on Compass’ 30-day VWAP as of September 19, 2025. Post-close, Compass shareholders will own about 78% of the merged company, while Anywhere shareholders will hold 22%. The deal, unanimously approved by both boards, is expected to close in the second half of 2026, pending customary approvals.

column6: TEGNA Acquisition by Nexstar Media Group Inc
column9: Nexstar Media Group (NASDAQ: NXST) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire TEGNA Inc. (NYSE: TEGNA) in an all-cash transaction valued at $6.2 billion, including TEGNA’s net debt and estimated transaction expenses. Under the terms of the agreement, Nexstar will pay $22.00 per share, representing a 31% premium to TEGNA’s 30-day average share price as of August 8, 2025—the last trading day prior to media speculation about a potential deal. The transaction has been unanimously approved by TEGNA’s board of directors, and TEGNA’s existing debt will be refinanced or assumed at closing. Nexstar has secured committed financing from BofA Securities, JPMorgan Chase, and Goldman Sachs to fund the deal. Closing is expected in the second half of 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

column6: Teck Resources Acquisition by Anglo American plc
column9: Anglo American plc and Teck Resources Limited have agreed to merge, creating Anglo Teck, a new global critical minerals giant and one of the world’s top five copper producers, headquartered in Canada. The merger will be executed through a Canadian plan of arrangement, with each Teck share (Class A or B) exchanged for 1.3301 Anglo American shares, or for eligible Canadian holders, an equivalent number of exchangeable shares in a Canadian Anglo subsidiary. Post-closing, Anglo American shareholders will own ~62.4% of the new company and Teck shareholders ~37.6%. To balance ownership and establish a clean capital structure, Anglo American will pay a US$4.5 billion special dividend (about US$4.19 per share) to its shareholders before completion. Anglo Teck plans listings on the LSE, JSE, TSX, and NYSE (via ADRs). The merger requires approval from Teck shareholders (two-thirds of both Class A and B shares voting separately), Canadian court approval, regulatory approvals, and is expected to close in 12–18 months.

column6: Electronic Arts Acquisition by Silver Lake, Public Investment Fund (PIF), Affinity Partners
column9: Electronic Arts (NASDAQ: EA) has agreed to be acquired by a consortium consisting of Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF), Silver Lake, and Affinity Partners in an all-cash transaction valuing the gaming giant at $55 billion, marking the largest sponsor-led take-private deal in history; EA shareholders will receive $210 per share, a 25% premium to its unaffected September 25, 2025 close, while PIF will roll over its existing 9.9% stake, with the $36 billion equity contribution split among the consortium members and $20 billion in fully committed debt financing from JPMorgan, of which $18 billion will be funded at close, and EA will remain headquartered in Redwood City under CEO Andrew Wilson, with the transaction expected to close in Q1 FY27.

column6: Norfolk Southern Acquisition by Union Pacific
column9: Union Pacific (NYSE: UNP) will acquire Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) in a stock-and-cash transaction valuing NSC at $320 per share, a 25% premium to its 30-day VWAP as of July 16, 2025. NSC shareholders will receive 1.0 UNP share and $88.82 in cash per NSC share, implying an $85B enterprise value for NSC. The combined company will be valued at over $250B and create America’s first transcontinental railroad spanning 50,000+ miles and 100 ports.

column6: Bright Scholar Education Acquisition by Bright Scholar Education Management
column9: Bright Scholar has received a preliminary non-binding proposal from its Chairperson Hongru Zhou, CEO Ruolei Niu, and affiliated shareholders (the "Buyer Group") to take the company private at $0.50 per Class A share ($2.00 per ADS), reflecting a 28.2% premium to the prior closing price. The proposed all-cash transaction is to be funded through a mix of equity and/or debt. The board has announced plans to form a special committee of independent directors to evaluate the offer, supported by external financial and legal advisors. The offer remains subject to negotiation and customary closing conditions, with no assurance that a definitive agreement will be reached.

column6: SWK Holdings Acquisition by Runway Growth Finance
column9: Runway Growth Finance Corp. (NASDAQ: RWAY) announced a definitive merger agreement to acquire SWK Holdings Corporation (NASDAQ: SWKH), a specialty finance company focused on providing non-dilutive capital to commercial-stage healthcare firms. The deal, structured as a net asset value (NAV)-for-NAV merger, is valued at approximately $220 million based on SWK’s June 30, 2025 financials, inclusive of estimated transaction expenses. Consideration will comprise roughly $75.5 million in Runway Growth shares, valued at closing NAV per share, and approximately $145 million in cash, which will be adjusted to reflect SWK’s final NAV calculated 48 hours before closing. In addition, Runway Growth Capital LLC, the company’s external adviser, will contribute an extra $9 million in cash to be distributed directly to SWK shareholders.

column6: Comerica Acquisition by Fifth Third Bancorp
column9: Fifth Third Bancorp (Nasdaq: FITB) announced a $10.9 billion all-stock acquisition of Comerica Incorporated (NYSE: CMA), under which Comerica shareholders will receive 1.8663 Fifth Third shares for each Comerica share, equating to $82.88 per share and a 20% premium to Comerica’s 10-day VWAP. Post-transaction, Fifth Third shareholders will own 73% and Comerica shareholders 27% of the combined company, which will become the 9th largest U.S. bank with approximately $288 billion in assets. The merger is expected to be immediately accretive, delivering peer-leading efficiency, strong returns on assets and tangible common equity, and establishing a platform for sustainable long-term growth.

column6: Semler Scientific Acquisition by Strive
column9: Strive, Inc. announced it will acquire Semler Scientific, Inc. in an all-stock transaction valuing Semler at a 210% premium, or about $90.52 per share, with each Semler share to be exchanged for 21.05 Strive Class A shares. Alongside the deal, Strive purchased 5,816 Bitcoin at an average of $116,047, totaling $675 million, boosting its treasury to 5,886 BTC. Post-merger, the combined company will hold over 10,900 Bitcoin (excluding future financings) and plans to consider monetizing or distributing Semler’s profitable diagnostics business while expanding into preventative diagnostics under new management.

column6: Soho House Take Private by MCR
column9: Soho House & Co Inc. (NYSE: SHCO) entered into definitive agreements under which an investor group led by MCR and its Chairman & CEO Tyler Morse will acquire all outstanding shares not held by certain significant shareholders. Common shareholders will receive $9.00 per share in cash, representing an 83% premium to SHCO’s closing price on December 18, 2024, the day prior to the company’s receipt of the initial offer. The deal implies a total enterprise value of approximately $2.7 billion. SHCO Executive Chairman Ron Burkle and The Yucaipa Companies will roll their controlling equity interests and retain majority control of the business post-close. The transaction is supported by funds managed by Apollo, which are providing a hybrid capital solution comprising both debt and common equity; a portion of the proceeds will be used to refinance SHCO’s existing Senior Secured Notes.

column6: CyberArk Acquisition by Palo Alto Networks
column9: Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ: PANW) will acquire CyberArk (NASDAQ: CYBR) for $45.00 in cash and 2.2005 PANW shares per CYBR share, implying a total equity value of ~$25 billion and a 26% premium to CYBR's 10-day VWAP as of July 25, 2025. The transaction has been unanimously approved by both boards and is expected to close in the second half of PANW's fiscal 2026, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals.

column6: CoreCard Acquisition by Euronet
column9: Euronet (NASDAQ: EEFT) will acquire CoreCard (NYSE: CCRD) in a stock-for-stock transaction valuing CoreCard at approximately $248 million, or $30.00 per share. CoreCard shareholders will receive between 0.2783 and 0.3142 shares of Euronet stock per share, subject to a collar based on Euronet's 15-day VWAP ($95.48 floor, $107.80 ceiling). The deal is expected to close in late 2025, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals.

column6: Anebulo Pharmaceuticals MBO
column9: Anebulo Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: ANEB) announced that its board, following a Special Committee recommendation, approved a going-private transaction structured as a reverse stock split in which holders of fewer than 2,500–7,500 shares (depending on the ratio selected) will be cashed out at $3.50 per pre-split share, representing a 91% premium to the July 22, 2025 close, while larger holders will retain one post-split share per Minimum Number of pre-split shares plus cash for any fractional shares; the company cited the high cost of public reporting relative to its drug development strategy as the rationale for the transaction.

column6: Protagenic Therapeutics Reverse Merger with Phytanix
column9: Protagenic Therapeutics (Nasdaq: PTIX) and Phytanix Bio have agreed to merge in an all-stock reverse merger transaction, forming a combined company to be named Phytanix, Inc. The new entity will focus on central nervous system and stress-related disorders, integrating five preclinical programs and one clinical-stage asset. Under the terms of the share exchange agreement, Phytanix Bio shareholders will receive newly issued shares of Protagenic on a pro rata basis, resulting in post-transaction ownership of approximately 65% for Phytanix Bio shareholders and 35% for legacy Protagenic shareholders, on a fully diluted basis.

column2: Hall of Fame Resort & Entertainment Company
column6: Hall of Fame Resort & Entertainment Company Acquisition by HOFV Holdings (Industrial Realty Group)
column9: Hall of Fame Resort & Entertainment Company (Nasdaq: HOFV/HOFVW) has agreed to be taken private in a cash-out merger at $0.90 per share by HOFV Holdings LLC, an affiliate of Industrial Realty Group led by director Stuart Lichter. The transaction, which will delist the company, is subject to approval by a majority of outstanding shares, $20 million of financing for the investor, completion of a lease restructuring, at least $125 million of project-level financing, and various third-party consents. A sale-leaseback arrangement for certain properties is also planned.

column6: Staffing 360 Solutions Acquisition by Atlantic International Corp.
column9: Staffing 360 Solutions ($STAF) to be acquired by Atlantic International Corp. ($ATLN) in an All Stock deal, with STAF holders to receive 1.2020 ATLN shares per STAF share, with a transaction value of appx. $25M.
